blocks.content: Shirone continued his relentless assault, swinging his blades blindly.
Gaold, calmly dodging each attack, smirked.

blocks.speaker: Gaold
blocks.content: Correct. He got the first question right.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Shirone wasn't holding back his magic; he simply couldn't use it.
Gaold had only one chance to attack, but that one chance would almost certainly kill Shirone.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Attack magic is out! I have to block it no matter what!
If Gaold was serious, he wouldn't wait for Ataraxia to be completed.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Shirone would die before that. Thus, all his attacks were feints. Instead, he focused all his attention on Gaold's reactions.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: When? When will it be?
Parallel processing allowed him to complete two spells simultaneously, but there was no room to equip Photon Cannon in the remaining slot besides Ataraxia.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: ...That's what he's thinking.
It was as if Shirone's thoughts were audible.
It was the best judgment, but there was one miscalculation: assuming the power and speed of the Air Gun would be the same as before.

blocks.speaker: Gaold
blocks.content: Did you really think you could block it? That's a bit unfortunate.
Gaold had no intention of going easy on Shirone.
He had given him two chances and set a rule allowing only one counterattack.
With this balance, Shirone's survival was entirely up to him.
Just before Ataraxia was completed, Gaold extended his finger.

blocks.speaker: Gaold
blocks.content: Goodbye, Shirone.
At the same time, Armand, waiting to respond to a single reaction, struck the ground with its tendrils and retreated.
But that was also within Gaold's expectations.

blocks.speaker: Gaold
blocks.content: This will be on a different level.
Without giving the same leeway as before, Gaold compressed the air and fired the Air Gun.
The air bullet flew at an incredible speed with a sound like shattering metal.
Shirone couldn't see or feel it.
That was the limit of a human who couldn't open the schema.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: But he had another escape route: imagination.
He had pre-designed a binary choice.
If Gaold attacked before Ataraxia was completed, it meant he intended to kill him.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Dark Sphere!
A black sphere formed in front of Shirone's face.
It was a spell he couldn't reach even through Behemoth's forced rebound, but with the power of mental transcendence, a small gravity field was created.
The Air Gun, aimed at his forehead, was sucked into the darkness with a whoosh.
Although he had a record of consuming a ton of plants in Kazra, Gaold's Air Gun was on a different level.
The black sphere, unable to handle the energy, exploded with a shockwave in front of Shirone's eyes.
Ugh!
Armand's robe instantly hardened to protect its master.
Shirone, blown away by the impact, planted his tendrils into the ground and leaped sideways.
Ataraxia, now completed, was shining brilliantly.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Magic Amplification!
Shirone extended his hands, focusing on the two magic amplification orbs in his palms.
A powerful Photon Cannon was born, creating small bubbles of light around it.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Now, Immortal Function!
The Photon Cannon rapidly expanded, pushing the air around it.
The golden light vibrated wildly, leaving afterimages on the onlookers' retinas.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Mental Transcendence!
As he pushed the mass to its limit, the photon mass, as large as a human torso, shook violently enough to be seen with the naked eye.
The noise of the mass's chaotic rotation was louder than thunder.
Flu's face turned pale.
By the rules, Gaold could no longer attack Shirone.
Conversely, Shirone's Photon Cannon was far more powerful than the first casting.

blocks.speaker: Flu
blocks.content: How can he compress light to that extent?

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Shirone, still unsatisfied, furrowed his brow even more.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: A little more! Just a little more...!
Gaold couldn't attack him anyway.
He would either dodge or block it.
So, he had to make it impossible to dodge.
Ahhhhh!
Pouring everything he had, a white sphere reminiscent of a supernova was born.
It was amplified to the extreme through Ataraxia.
The entire bunker would likely be filled with energy.

blocks.speaker: Gaold
blocks.content: Damn it...
Flash!
A massive wave of light pushed out in all directions.
Even Kangnan's group, standing behind Ataraxia, turned their heads from the blinding flash that could burn their retinas.
The shape was visible even with their eyes closed.
But Shirone could see.
Thanks to Armand blocking 98% of the light entering his eyes.
Then, a déjà vu-like scene unfolded before him.
The edges of the light quickly tightened, and the unimaginable atmospheric pressure trapped the Photon Cannon's energy in the Vacuum Press.
Ughhhh!
Gaold grimaced and tightened the Vacuum Press even more.
Finally, the energy stabilized, gathering into a small sphere of light no larger than his palm.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Shirone was stunned.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Is that man... really human?
Gaold, bent over like a beast, let his arms hang down.
His hair was completely bleached white.
As he clenched his fist, the Vacuum Press burst, and the Photon Cannon vanished.

blocks.speaker: Gaold
blocks.content: Whew. That was close.
Shirone wasn't the only one surprised.
Even Flu and Gando, who had been assisting Gaold, had never seen his hair turn white.
Gaold remained bent over for a long time.
Then he looked at Shirone and smiled.

blocks.speaker: Gaold
blocks.content: ...You pass.
He's already fainted.
Kangnan adjusted his glasses and walked to the center of the bunker.
Shirone lay on the ground as if dead.
Although using Ataraxia no longer caused him to lose consciousness, this demonstration was undoubtedly his best effort.

blocks.speaker: Gaold
blocks.content: Heh, he really gave it his all. Well, fine.
Gaold shook his numb arm a few times, then picked up the cord on the floor and slung it over his shoulder.
Kangnan stared at Gaold's bleached hair.
This Vacuum Press had been Gaold's sincere effort.
Hence, Shirone passed.

blocks.speaker: Kangnan
blocks.content: You should dye your hair. It looks even older now.

blocks.speaker: Gaold
blocks.content: Leave it. It'll grow back. Isn't bleaching trendy these days?

blocks.speaker: Kangnan
blocks.content: So I've heard. But your face isn't trendy, Chairman.

blocks.speaker: Gaold
blocks.content: Hahaha!
Gaold laughed heartily and headed for the elevator.
As he pressed the button, he turned to Flu.

blocks.speaker: Gaold
blocks.content: Hey, kid.
Flu, tense, stood at attention and answered.
She thought she knew Gaold well after two years, but she was mistaken.
The Gaold she knew wasn't the real one.

blocks.speaker: Gaold
blocks.content: Take him and let him rest. Bring him to my room when he wakes up.
Yes! Understood!
That was all the interest Gaold showed in Flu.
Flu looked at him with sad eyes.
She had cried, snot and tears streaming, shouting her respect.
It was the greatest courage of her life.
But it seemed to have no effect on Gaold.
Or maybe it was already forgotten.

blocks.speaker: Gando
blocks.content: Flu.
Gando placed a hand on Flu's shoulder.

blocks.speaker: Gando
blocks.content: I'll take Shirone.

blocks.speaker: Flu
blocks.content: N-No, I should do it.
Men are heavy.
You can't use magic on the first floor.
Gando entered the bunker, lifted Shirone's arm, and skillfully turned his body to carry him on his back.
Flu, watching for a long time, heard Gando's voice.

blocks.speaker: Gando
blocks.content: You were impressive, Flu.
It didn't take long for Flu to understand Gando's words.
A thin smile appeared on her tearful face.

blocks.speaker: Flu
blocks.content: Thank you.
Shirone opened his eyes.
His head throbbed for a moment, but then he felt refreshed.
He was in the recovery room of the Magic Association.
The capsule he lay in had a glass ceiling and a cozy cushion.
As Shirone blinked a few times, the glass ceiling slid open with a clunk.
Flu's worried face appeared in the narrow view.

blocks.speaker: Flu
blocks.content: How are you? Feeling better?

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Ah, yes. What happened?

blocks.speaker: Flu
blocks.content: You lost consciousness in the bunker. That's what happens when you overuse magic.

blocks.speaker: Flu
blocks.content: Next time, even if it means reducing the Spirit Zone, increase the density.

blocks.speaker: Flu
blocks.content: It'll prevent brain overload.
Flu's voice was gentle, unlike before.
It felt awkward for Shirone, but he could see the sadness in her eyes, so he didn't show it.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Yes, thank you.
Shirone sat up.
Capsules like the one he was in were lined up in rows.
Most had their glass ceilings open, but someone was sleeping in a corner.

blocks.speaker: Flu
blocks.content: That's the duty officer.

blocks.speaker: Flu
blocks.content: Sleep capsules enhance mental activity.

blocks.speaker: Flu
blocks.content: But don't overdo it.

blocks.speaker: Flu
blocks.content: They're not perfect.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: How long has it been?

blocks.speaker: Flu
blocks.content: Two hours.

blocks.speaker: Flu
blocks.content: Your recovery is faster than expected.

blocks.speaker: Flu
blocks.content: I thought it would take six hours.
Shirone, satisfied with his mental endurance, stretched his body.
Then he glanced at Flu.
There was an inexplicable distance between them compared to a few hours ago.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Um...

blocks.speaker: Flu
blocks.content: Follow me. The Chairman is looking for you.
Before he could speak, Flu walked towards the exit.
Shirone followed her to the elevator.

blocks.speaker: Flu
blocks.content: In case you didn't hear, you passed.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Thank you.

blocks.speaker: Flu
blocks.content: You don't need to thank me.

blocks.speaker: Flu
blocks.content: You'll be going to heaven with the Chairman.

blocks.speaker: Flu
blocks.content: From now on, Shirone would be privy to top-level secrets she could never access.

blocks.speaker: Flu
blocks.content: There was no need to be cautious anymore.

blocks.speaker: Flu
blocks.content: And we'll be the second string.

blocks.speaker: Flu
blocks.content: Neither I nor Gando will directly assist Gaold.

blocks.speaker: Flu
blocks.content: It must be an incredibly high-level operation.

blocks.speaker: Flu
blocks.content: I don't know exactly what the mission is, but Gaold said it's a project he's dedicated his life to.

blocks.speaker: Flu
blocks.content: If there are collaborators, they'll be the best mages, and it will be an elite group.

blocks.speaker: Flu
blocks.content: You're part of that.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Shirone felt a pang of guilt, realizing what Flu was implying.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: I'm sorry.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: I didn't mean to upset you.

blocks.speaker: Flu
blocks.content: Ataraxia was a secret Shirone had mastered at the risk of his life.

blocks.speaker: Flu
blocks.content: But there were many mages in the world willing to risk their lives to reach a higher level.

blocks.speaker: Flu
blocks.content: He couldn't claim superiority.

blocks.speaker: Flu
blocks.content: Don't think that way.

blocks.speaker: Flu
blocks.content: You deserve the Chairman's recognition.
Hearing this from Flu felt different than hearing it from anyone else.
The time they had spent bickering as mentor and mentee flashed before his eyes.
Feeling Shirone's sweet gaze, Flu recoiled with a disgusted expression.
Quickly regaining her usual demeanor, she pointed at him as if striking an enemy breaching the defense line.

blocks.speaker: Flu
blocks.content: But you're not there yet!

blocks.speaker: Flu
blocks.content: While Ataraxia's power is impressive, you need to refine it to mitigate the risks.

blocks.speaker: Flu
blocks.content: A true mage can handle countless unpredictable situations.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Yes.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: I'll do my best, senpai.

blocks.speaker: Flu
blocks.content: Hmph!
The elevator doors opened with a clear electronic chime.
